text:
Jack loves camping. He loves the woods. There's something about the quiet. There's something about not being able to touch anyone except his wife.

It was the third weekend in June. Their tent was perched at the edge of a lake. They were early.

"Time for breakfast," Jack said.

He made pancakes. They ate around the campfire. She smiled at him.

The birds chirped. They played cards.

The sun climbed high. The sky turned pink. They got sleepy.

"You need to come home," she said.

"Why?" he asked.

"There's stuff outside."

She slipped out.

He was asleep by dawn. Quiet. Empty.

Jack looked up. She was standing.

"Why are you here?"

"You're supposed to look after your wife."

He chuckled faintly.

"I'm good." A good man, she said approvingly.

They walked back towards camp.

That night, he dreamed about a mountain.

The next morning, they packed. They left early.
